definition: With services comprising ~85% of nonfarm payrolls and manufacturing <10% of S&P 500 by weight, labor cost is the deterministic driver of aggregate profitability. Traditional goods-sector margin frameworks (commodity inputs, factory utilization) apply to a shrinking share of the economy.
open_questions: Could AI/automation shift this faster than the gradual pace assumed?
How does the services dominance interact with remote work effects?
